content:
6 min read
4 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

For nine years, you have been writing the same line in every component.
changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush
Every senior review demanded it. Every performance article preached it. Every good Angular developer typed it on autopilot, thousands of times, across thousands of components.
On June 3, 2026, Angular 22 shipped and quietly deleted the need for it.
OnPush is now the default. The strategy you have been manually opting into since 2016 is simply what Angular does. And the old behavior did not just lose its throne, it lost its name. It is now called Eager, and it sits in your codebase like a to-do list.
That is shift number one. There are four more, and at least two of them can break your app in ways your tests will not catch. Pour the coffee. Let us go through all five like adults.
Shift 1: OnPush Is the Default (The Big One)
For nine years, Angular checked every component on every tick unless you said otherwise. The framework assumed everything changed, everywhere, all the time.
old default (Eager): any event ---> check root…
